text:
The paper presents an analysis of the spatial structure and the level of development of Polish agriculture on the basis of the 2010 National Agricultural Census (NAC) data. The internal features of the agricultural sector were classified into six diagnostic blocks as follows: i) quality and use of agricultural land, ii) agrarian structure, iii) socio-demographic aspects, iv) technical means, v) production structure, and vi) production and economic efficiency. A synthetic index of agriculture was built-up in order to assess the overall level of agriculture at the level of 314 regional offices of the Agency for Restructuring and Modernisation of Agriculture (ARiMR).

The results indicate a very high degree of differentiation in spatial terms at the level of provinces and counties. These relations were presented against the background of four external factors: natural conditions, historical and political situation, level of urbanization and the level of implementation of CAP funds. The results of the analysis showed that the impact of natural environment conditions is relatively minor, and that the non-natural factors – primarily the historical borders and the degree of using EU funding for rural development – have a significant impact on shaping the current state of agriculture in Poland. The third aspect which was determined in the research is the locations of agricultural development problems. This particularly refers to the agricultural structures in south-eastern part of the country (in Małopolskie and Podkarpackie provinces) where the most diversified agricultural structure and the lowest level of its modernization were noted. Historically determined factors are also still a deciding factor in the spatial differentiation of agriculture in Poland.
